name: Substack
longDescription:
Substack is a publishing platform built around email newsletters and paid subscriptions. Writers create their own publication, publish posts that go out by email and live on the web, and optionally charge readers a monthly or yearly fee for full access.

It handles the operational pieces independent writers usually have to wire up themselves: hosting, mailing lists, payments via Stripe, comments, and a reader app. Beyond text, Substack supports podcasts, video, and short-form Notes, and recommendations between publications help writers find new readers.

Substack is used by journalists, analysts, novelists, and hobbyists to run direct-to-reader publications without a traditional outlet. Reading is free for anyone with the link, but paying subscribers unlock paywalled posts and any subscriber-only community features.

features.title: Email + web publishing
features.description: Every post is delivered to subscribers' inboxes and lives on a clean web page at the same time.

features.title: Paid subscriptions
features.description: Writers can charge monthly or yearly with payments, taxes, and renewals handled through Stripe.

features.title: Podcasts and video
features.description: Publications can include podcast episodes and video posts alongside written articles.

features.title: Notes and recommendations
features.description: Short-form Notes and cross-publication recommendations help writers grow their audience.

features.title: Reader app and community
features.description: A dedicated mobile app surfaces subscriptions, comments, and chat threads with subscribers.

useCases: Running an independent newsletter as a journalist or analyst
Charging readers directly for in-depth or niche writing
Distributing a podcast or video series with email follow-ups
Building a small paid community around a topic or beat
Migrating an existing mailing list to a hosted platform

faq.question: Is Substack free for writers?
faq.answer: Substack is free to start; the company takes a 10% fee on paid subscriptions plus standard Stripe payment processing fees.

faq.question: Do readers need an account?
faq.answer: Reading public posts only needs an email address, but commenting, paying for a subscription, or using the app requires a free Substack account.

faq.question: Can I export my subscriber list?
faq.answer: Yes, writers own their subscriber list and email addresses and can export them at any time to move to another platform.

faq.question: Can I run a podcast on Substack?
faq.answer: Yes, Substack supports podcast hosting with private feeds for paying subscribers and public feeds for free episodes.
